preamble: The fronting of the voiced uvular plosive [ɢ] to the voiced velar plosive [ɡ] is a shift that moves the point of articulation forward. This transformation often reflects a simplification of the consonant inventory or a systemic move away from uvular sounds toward more common velar ones.

examples.note: Historical voiced uvular sounds have merged with the velar [ɡ] in many modern Persian dialects.
Reconstructed historical stages of Tibetic languages often show uvular stops fronting to velar stops.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
